raw_response: {"response":"Reject as-is. The proposed workflow aligns with the general webhook debugging domain, but it should not be trusted because the test-event outcome is not treated as first-class evidence and the flow makes the work feel like a linear progression instead of diagnostic comparison. The engineer must be able to compare endpoint status, retry history, selected request, test result, error cause, and candidate fix in one working context before choosing the next repair.","next_action":"Repair it as a setup debug workspace: add test-event result to the core evidence, keep selected request context visible across retries and tests, place remediation controls beside the evidence they depend on, use apply/record language for the next fix, and move non-user-facing machinery into diagnostic-only detail.","questions":[],"handoff":"Decision: reject as-is; owner: workflow revision agent; action: repair evidence continuity and disclosure; reason: the fix decision is missing explicit test-result evidence and has unnecessary linear friction; verification: platform engineer can choose the next fix from visible endpoint, retry, request, test-result, and error-cause context.","rationale":"Webhook setup debugging is an evidence-led troubleshooting activity. Request IDs and retry details are appropriate in this setup context, but implementation machinery should not become primary user-facing language. The repaired workflow should preserve decision-changing dependencies at the point of action."}

parsed.response: Reject as-is. The proposed workflow aligns with the general webhook debugging domain, but it should not be trusted because the test-event outcome is not treated as first-class evidence and the flow makes the work feel like a linear progression instead of diagnostic comparison. The engineer must be able to compare endpoint status, retry history, selected request, test result, error cause, and candidate fix in one working context before choosing the next repair.
parsed.next_action: Repair it as a setup debug workspace: add test-event result to the core evidence, keep selected request context visible across retries and tests, place remediation controls beside the evidence they depend on, use apply/record language for the next fix, and move non-user-facing machinery into diagnostic-only detail.
parsed.handoff: Decision: reject as-is; owner: workflow revision agent; action: repair evidence continuity and disclosure; reason: the fix decision is missing explicit test-result evidence and has unnecessary linear friction; verification: platform engineer can choose the next fix from visible endpoint, retry, request, test-result, and error-cause context.
parsed.rationale: Webhook setup debugging is an evidence-led troubleshooting activity. Request IDs and retry details are appropriate in this setup context, but implementation machinery should not become primary user-facing language. The repaired workflow should preserve decision-changing dependencies at the point of action.

execution.command_display: codex exec --model gpt-5.5 -c model_reasoning_effort="xhigh" --sandbox read-only --skip-git-repo-check --ephemeral --color never --output-schema <schema> --output-last-message <output> -
